title: NOC 92010 – Supervisors, mineral and metal processing | GTR Immigration
description: Supervisors under NOC 92010 are the technical leaders of Canada's smelting, refining, and metal forming industries. As a TEER 2 occupation, these professionals oversee the conversion of raw ores into high-value metals like nickel, copper, and aluminum—all of which are critical to the global green energy transition in 2026. With Canada’s "Critical Minerals Strategy" in full swing, these supervisors are essential for managing high-tech, low-emission processing plants. For immigration, this role is a prime candidate for Express Entry Category-Based Selection and the Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program (OINP).

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category9 – Occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesTEER Level2Major Group92 – Processing, manufacturing and utilities supervisorsSub-Major Group920 – Processing, manufacturing and utilities supervisorsMinor Group9201 – Supervisors, processing and manufacturing occupationsNOC Code92010

common_job_titles: Refining Supervisor - Mineral and Metal Processing
duties: Supervise, coordinate, and schedule the activities of workers who operate furnaces, refining equipment, and metal rolling mills.
Establish work schedules and procedures and coordinate activities with other departments to ensure seamless production.
Resolve work problems and recommend measures to improve productivity and product quality.
Requisition materials and supplies and manage equipment maintenance schedules to prevent downtime.
Train workers in job duties, safety procedures, and company policies, especially regarding the handling of hazardous materials.
Ensure strict adherence to environmental regulations and occupational health and safety (OHS) standards.
Prepare detailed production and safety reports for senior plant management.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Work Experience: Several years of experience in the specific processing or manufacturing occupation supervised are mandatory.
Education: A college diploma or university degree in metallurgy, chemical engineering, or a related field is increasingly preferred for modern automated plants.
Certifications: Internal company certifications and provincial safety designations (e.g., Joint Health and Safety Committee) are often required.
Strong leadership, mechanical aptitude, and proficiency in digital production management software are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: Supervisors in remote "Fly-In-Fly-Out" (FIFO) refining sites in Northern Canada often earn significantly more when including remote site premiums and performance bonuses.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Industry data suggests that the push for "Critical Minerals" (Nickel, Lithium, Cobalt) will create a 12% growth in processing supervisor positions by 2033. Replacement demand due to the retirement of senior plant staff remains the primary driver of new openings, particularly in the traditional smelting hubs of Ontario and Quebec.
labour_market_demand: The demand for mineral and metal processing supervisors is Good to Very Good, particularly in provinces with established mining and refining hubs. In 2026, Ontario (Sudbury/Niagara), Quebec (Saguenay–Lac-Saint-Jean), and British Columbia are reporting a high need for experienced supervisors to manage automated smelting operations and "Green Steel" production lines. As the industry shifts toward circular economy models (metal recycling), supervisors with experience in secondary processing are in exceptionally high demand.
Leader in Sustainable Metallurgy: Lead operations in a country at the forefront of carbon-neutral aluminum and steel production.
Technological Advancement: Manage AI-driven furnace controls, IoT-enabled refining processes, and advanced hydrometallurgical extraction techniques.
High Earning Potential: As a TEER 2 supervisory role in a high-value resource sector, salaries are among the most competitive in Canadian manufacturing.
Stable PR Pathways: Highly valued in the Federal Skilled Trades Program and specific Express Entry draws for Trade and STEM occupations.
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Targeted for candidates with experience in "Trade Occupations."
OINP Employer Job Offer (Foreign Worker Stream): A direct route for those with a job offer from an Ontario-based refining or smelting firm.
BC PNP Skills Immigration: Priority for supervisors supporting the province’s vital mineral processing sector.
Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): A reliable pathway for supervisors with the required work experience and a job offer.
